V-M-074.scope: Responsive adaptation verification for phone, tablet, and desktop layouts across public, auth, user-cabinet, and admin Matrix surfaces.
V-M-074.verification-intent: Phase-61 must prove that the premium Matrix UI is intentionally designed for mobile and tablet use, not merely tolerated by desktop CSS.

commands.command: python3 -m xml.etree.ElementTree docs/verification-index.xml docs/verification/V-M-074.xml docs/modules/M-074.xml docs/plans/Phase-61.xml
git diff --name-only -- backend deploy docker-compose.yml install.sh mtproto-runtime official-mtproxy nginx telegram-bot is empty
cd frontend && npm run build; node scripts/phase67-auth-splash-smoke.mjs; node scripts/phase61-responsive-adaptation-smoke.mjs; node scripts/phase60-premium-qa-smoke.mjs
cd frontend && npm run build; cd frontend-admin && npm run build; node scripts/phase61-responsive-adaptation-smoke.mjs; Chrome 390x844 user/admin screenshot and bottom-pixel sanity
cd frontend-admin && npm run build; node scripts/phase74-admin-shell-navigation-smoke.mjs passed; node scripts/phase61-responsive-adaptation-smoke.mjs remains a final-run regression gate
cd frontend-admin && npm run build; node scripts/phase75-admin-users-devices-smoke.mjs; Phase-61 responsive smoke remains a final-run regression gate

evidence.item: 2026-06-04: frontend npm run build passed; primary JS 448870 bytes gzip 135741, primary CSS 44212 bytes gzip 8832.
2026-06-04: frontend-admin npm run build passed; primary JS 780907 bytes gzip 219491, primary CSS 39548 bytes gzip 8687. Vite large-chunk warning remains inside the Phase-61 940KB admin budget.

2026-06-04: scripts/phase61-responsive-adaptation-smoke.mjs passed and emitted BUILDS_PASS, VIEWPORT_MATRIX_READY, PHONE_LAYOUT_PASS, TABLET_LAYOUT_PASS, DESKTOP_REGRESSION_PASS, NO_HORIZONTAL_SCROLL, NO_OVERLAP_PASS, TOUCH_TARGETS_PASS, SAFE_AREA_PASS, PROTECTED_USER_STATIC_PROOF, ADMIN_STATIC_PROOF, REDUCED_MOTION_PASS, BUNDLE_BUDGET_PASS, and PROTECTED_SURFACE_GUARD.
2026-06-04: scripts/phase60-premium-qa-smoke.mjs passed after the Phase-61 implementation commit and preserved Phase-52/55/56/57/58/59 regression smokes.
2026-06-04: XML parse, MyGRACE lint, git diff --check, and protected backend/deploy/runtime guard passed.

2026-06-04: Authenticated protected-route browser screenshots are not claimed locally; Phase-61 records static-route proof and leaves real backend-session screenshot review for clean-deploy validation.

2026-06-04: Phase-62 compactness/deletion audit preserved Phase-61 responsive compactness markers after folding secondary dashboard/config/subscription/public/admin surfaces; user/admin builds and Phase-61/60 regressions remained green.

2026-06-04: Phase-67 auth/splash responsive smoke and browser screenshots under /tmp/krotpn-phase67-shots passed for splash redirect, large unframed logo, red-focus auth inputs, side-by-side secondary actions with narrow-phone wrapping, reduced-motion fallback, and protected backend/deploy/runtime guard.

2026-06-04: Rainfix responsive evidence passed: Phase-61 smoke now asserts .matrix-rain-fallback, min-height calc(100dvh + 128px), CSS_RAIN_FALLBACK_READY, and MOBILE_OVERSCAN_READY; 390x844 user/admin Chrome screenshots show no bottom black cutoff; bottom crop max values were user 0.246326 and admin 0.841062.
2026-06-04: Phase-68 dashboard-owned subscription panel passed frontend/admin builds, Phase-61 responsive smoke, Phase-60 QA, and compact calendar/tariff static guards.

2026-06-05: Phase-74 frontend-admin build and admin shell smoke passed for rail viewport bounds, content scroll surface, and no visible Tariffs nav; Phase-61 responsive smoke remains part of final regression suite.

2026-06-05: Phase-75 frontend-admin build and admin Users/Devices smoke passed for unified inventory no-overflow markers, bounded expanded device rows, mobile reachable device actions, and protected backend/deploy/runtime/user frontend guard.

viewport-matrix.phone-1: 320x568 proves smallest supported phone does not clip auth/public CTAs or cabinet actions.
viewport-matrix.phone-2: 360x740 proves compact Android layout, bottom navigation spacing, forms, and MTProto/VPN cards.
viewport-matrix.phone-3: 375x667 proves legacy iPhone-height constraints without hidden primary controls.
viewport-matrix.phone-4: 390x844 proves common iPhone layout, safe-area padding, calendar/subscription and config panels.
viewport-matrix.phone-5: 430x932 proves large phone density without oversized hero, cards, or chart frames.
viewport-matrix.tablet-1: 768x1024 proves portrait tablet layout has intentional two-column or bounded single-column behavior.
viewport-matrix.tablet-2: 820x1180 proves iPad-like portrait layout preserves readable public, cabinet, and admin surfaces.
viewport-matrix.tablet-3: 1024x768 proves landscape tablet does not become cramped desktop or stretched phone layout.
viewport-matrix.tablet-4: 1024x1366 proves tall tablet keeps navigation, charts, lists, and forms within predictable bounds.
viewport-matrix.desktop-1: 1280x720 proves short desktop remains compact and primary content is not pushed below useful view.
viewport-matrix.desktop-2: 1440x900 proves premium desktop regression remains stable after mobile/tablet changes.
viewport-matrix.reduced-motion-1: 390x844 with reduced motion proves Matrix and route transitions remain readable.
viewport-matrix.reduced-motion-2: 768x1024 with reduced motion proves tablet UI remains usable without animated cues.

route-matrix.auth-limitation-policy: If local auth fixtures cannot enter protected routes, the smoke must produce static-route proof and record the remaining live screenshot gap instead of claiming false coverage.

phase-74-responsive-gates.scope: Phase-74 updates the admin route/navigation matrix by removing visible /plans/Tariffs from admin UI while preserving responsive shell safety.
phase-74-responsive-gates.gate-1: Admin shell at phone/tablet/desktop widths must keep Dashboard, Users, MTProto, Servers/Nodes, Analytics, and Logout reachable without horizontal scroll.
phase-74-responsive-gates.gate-2: Logout under Nodes and bounded rail geometry must remain readable at 360x740, 390x844, 430x932, 768x1024, 1024x768, and 1440x900.
phase-74-responsive-gates.gate-3: Admin content wheel/touch scroll must work over the content surface and must not be blocked by fixed Matrix backgrounds, overlays, or rail overflow.
phase-74-responsive-gates.gate-4: Phase-61 responsive smoke remains a required regression gate after the Phase-74 admin shell change.

phase-75-responsive-gates.scope: Responsive verification for merging admin Users and Devices into one expandable inventory.
phase-75-responsive-gates.gate-1: Admin /users must show a compact expandable device inventory without horizontal scroll on phone, tablet, desktop, and reduced-motion viewport classes.
phase-75-responsive-gates.gate-2: The removed visible /devices nav must not leave an unreachable device workflow; direct /devices must redirect or present compatibility handling without duplicate primary inventory.
phase-75-responsive-gates.gate-3: Expanded user device sublists must be bounded, scrollable or paginated, and must not stretch the whole page for users with many devices.
phase-75-responsive-gates.gate-4: Device action controls and confirmation surfaces must remain 44px-touch-safe on mobile and must not overlap the bottom tabbar, rail, Matrix canvas, or scroll containers.
phase-75-responsive-gates.commands: cd frontend-admin && npm run build; node scripts/phase75-admin-users-devices-smoke.mjs; node scripts/phase61-responsive-adaptation-smoke.mjs remains a final-run regression gate

phase-67-responsive-gates.gate-1: Splash / and auth routes must be verified at phone, tablet, desktop, and reduced-motion viewports.
phase-67-responsive-gates.gate-2: Large unframed logo, inputs, password controls, secondary buttons, redirect status, and form errors must not overlap or cause horizontal scroll.
phase-67-responsive-gates.gate-3: Secondary auth actions placed side by side must wrap intentionally on narrow phones without clipped text or sub-44px controls.
phase-67-responsive-gates.gate-4: Reduced-motion must disable or simplify splash/auth animations without leaving users stuck before /login.

rainfix-responsive-gates.gate-1: Canvas and fallback layers must overrun the viewport by 128px and use 100dvh-aware CSS so mobile browser chrome cannot expose a black bottom strip.
rainfix-responsive-gates.gate-2: Phase-61 smoke must fail if Matrix rain fallback or overscan markers are removed from user/admin CSS.
rainfix-responsive-gates.gate-3: Mobile screenshot or pixel sanity must prove the bottom viewport crop is nonblank after a production preview build.

success-scenarios.scenario-1: All planned phone, tablet, desktop, and reduced-motion viewports render without document-level or route-level horizontal scroll.
success-scenarios.scenario-2: Public/auth surfaces keep the first-screen brand signal, CTA, email/password forms, password hint, and recovery paths visible and non-overlapping.
success-scenarios.scenario-3: User cabinet keeps VPN config, MTProto proxy links, subscription countdown/calendar, referrals, and settings actions reachable on phone and tablet.
success-scenarios.scenario-4: Admin cockpit keeps dashboard metrics, users/devices lists, MTProto analytics, abuse alerts, servers, plans, and analytics charts bounded and readable.
success-scenarios.scenario-5: Inputs, icons, placeholders, labels, badges, chart legends, copy buttons, and action buttons do not overlap or resize their containers unexpectedly.
success-scenarios.scenario-6: Touch targets for primary navigation and actions are at least 44px in the planned phone/tablet breakpoints unless the element is non-interactive text.
success-scenarios.scenario-7: Mobile bottom navigation, sticky headers, fixed backgrounds, Matrix canvas, and scroll containers do not cover primary content or block pointer/scroll gestures.
success-scenarios.scenario-8: Tablet layouts show deliberate density and hierarchy: no lazy stretched phone layout, no cramped desktop table, and no nested-card visual bulk.
success-scenarios.scenario-9: Reduced-motion mode disables or softens motion while preserving status feedback, Matrix atmosphere, route readability, and focus visibility.
success-scenarios.scenario-10: Phase-60 premium QA smoke remains green after responsive edits, proving no regression in visual policy, assets, motion, accessibility, or bundle budgets.

failure-scenarios.failure-1: Any viewport hides or clips registration, login, password reset, VPN config, MTProto link, subscription, admin health, or admin action paths.
failure-scenarios.failure-2: Any document/body/route container produces horizontal scroll beyond a 2px measurement tolerance.
failure-scenarios.failure-3: Any icon overlaps placeholder text, field value, label, badge, button text, or chart/table content.
failure-scenarios.failure-4: Any phone/tablet route requires unreasonable vertical scanning because repeated cards, headings, or decorative surfaces dominate useful controls.
failure-scenarios.failure-5: Any admin table/list grows without internal bounding or loses search/filter/action reachability on phones or tablets.
failure-scenarios.failure-6: Any responsive fix changes backend APIs, deploy/install scripts, docker-compose, nginx, mtproto-runtime, official-mtproxy, or live operational behavior.
failure-scenarios.failure-7: Any smoke output exposes secrets, tokens, real emails, raw config payloads, WireGuard keys, MTProto secrets, payment IDs, or operator infrastructure credentials.

telemetry-requirements.allowed: route path, viewport size, app surface name, selector label, measured width/height, horizontal-scroll delta, screenshot artifact path, bundle size, and anonymized failure code.
telemetry-requirements.forbidden: passwords, access tokens, refresh tokens, API keys, Resend/YooKassa secrets, WireGuard private keys, MTProto secrets, full proxy links, real emails, raw user IPs, payment identifiers, and server access credentials.
telemetry-requirements.retention: Local screenshot and smoke artifacts may be stored under /tmp/krotpn-phase61-* during verification and must not be committed.

performance-budgets.frontend-js: Built user frontend JavaScript should stay within the Phase-60 budget unless a justified delta is recorded.
performance-budgets.frontend-css: Built user frontend CSS should stay within the Phase-60 budget unless a justified delta is recorded.
performance-budgets.admin-js: Built admin frontend JavaScript should stay within the Phase-60 budget unless a justified delta is recorded.
performance-budgets.admin-css: Built admin frontend CSS should stay within the Phase-60 budget unless a justified delta is recorded.
performance-budgets.asset-policy: No new public visual asset may exceed 512KB without explicit documentation, and no single frontend asset may exceed 1.5MB.

phase-gates.gate-1: V-M-074 cannot move to pass-local until user/admin builds, Phase-61 smoke, Phase-60 regression smoke, XML parse, MyGRACE lint, git diff --check, and protected surface guard pass.
phase-gates.gate-2: V-M-074 cannot move to pass-local if any protected-route screenshots are unavailable without a truthful auth-fixture/live-gap note.
phase-gates.gate-3: Phase-61 execution must not modify deploy/install scripts or backend/runtime services; any required operational change must become a separate confirmed phase.
phase-gates.gate-4: Known live-only evidence must be documented in M-074 and V-M-074 rather than hidden behind generic PASS wording.
phase-gates.gate-5: Phase-62 compactness changes must preserve Phase-61 responsive no-horizontal-scroll, no-overlap, touch-target, safe-area, bounded-scroll, and reduced-motion guarantees; local evidence passed on 2026-06-04.
phase-gates.gate-6: Phase-67 auth/splash responsive gate must preserve Phase-61 responsive guarantees and may not change protected user/admin responsive contracts.
phase-gates.gate-7: Matrix rain reliability hotfix must preserve Phase-61 responsive guarantees while adding bottom-edge overscan proof for user/admin mobile shells.
phase-gates.gate-8: Phase-74 admin shell responsive gate passed for rail bounds and content-scroll markers; full Phase-61 regression remains required in the final verification run.
phase-gates.gate-9: Phase-75 passed unified /users inventory, bounded expanded device lists, safe /devices handling, and responsive no-overflow markers through frontend-admin build and Phase-75 smoke.

autonomous-execution-safety.safety-1: Edits are limited to frontend, frontend-admin, Phase-61 smoke, and MyGRACE documentation unless the operator approves a new phase.
autonomous-execution-safety.safety-2: Use deterministic smoke assertions before screenshot review; screenshots are evidence, not the only gate.
autonomous-execution-safety.safety-3: Prefer compact responsive CSS and existing components over adding new visual systems or business workflows.
autonomous-execution-safety.safety-4: Do not commit generated screenshots, dist artifacts, local secrets, or temporary Playwright state.

phase-68-verification-design.gate-1: Phone, tablet, desktop, and reduced-motion viewports must show MTProto first and subscription below without horizontal scroll.
phase-68-verification-design.gate-2: Tariff cards, compact calendar, countdown, QR/config links, and cabinet buttons must remain touch-safe and readable on 360x740, 390x844, 430x932, 768x1024, and 1440x900.
phase-68-verification-design.gate-3: Removed sections must not create blank black gaps over the Matrix rain background.
phase-68-verification-design.commands: node scripts/phase61-responsive-adaptation-smoke.mjs; node scripts/phase68-dashboard-subscription-smoke.mjs; browser screenshot review for /dashboard and /dashboard/config

phase-71-verification-design.scope: Responsive verification for selected-device master-detail and mobile sticky actions.
phase-71-verification-design.gate-1: Phone, tablet, desktop, and reduced-motion viewports must keep device list, selected detail, QR/.conf/Copy, and secondary destructive menu visible without horizontal scroll.
phase-71-verification-design.gate-2: Sticky mobile actions must not cover bottom navigation, QR modal, device rows, or confirmation controls.
phase-71-verification-design.gate-3: Logo, calendar pulse, and X close button must not overlap text or controls at 360x740, 390x844, 430x932, 768x1024, and 1440x900.

V-M-074.phase-71-evidence: Frontend build and Phase-71 smoke passed for compact selected-device UX and sticky-action markers; post-commit Phase-61 and Phase-62 protected regression guards passed cleanly.

phase-72-verification-design.scope: Responsive verification for mobile bottom user navigation and compact desktop calendar.
phase-72-verification-design.gate-1: Phone and tablet viewports must show the bottom navigation without document-level horizontal scroll or overlap with QR, config, subscription, referral, settings, or auth controls.
phase-72-verification-design.gate-2: Desktop viewports must show the smaller subscription calendar without broken wrapping, clipped dates, or unreadable range boundary markers.
phase-72-verification-design.gate-3: Reduced-motion mode must keep the bottom navigation and calendar state visible without transform-heavy reveal animations.
phase-72-verification-design.commands: node scripts/phase61-responsive-adaptation-smoke.mjs; node scripts/phase72-user-auth-navigation-smoke.mjs; browser screenshot review for /dashboard and /dashboard/settings

V-M-074.phase-72-evidence: Frontend/admin builds, Phase-61 responsive smoke, and Phase-72 smoke passed for bottom dock no-overlap, desktop calendar no-overflow, and protected admin/backend/deploy/runtime guard.

phase-74-verification-design.scope: Responsive verification for admin shell navigation simplification, scroll repair, and rail bounds.
phase-74-verification-design.gate-1: Admin Tariffs/Plans removal must not create a navigation gap, orphaned route state, or horizontal scroll on phone/tablet/desktop.
phase-74-verification-design.gate-2: Rail clipping and content scrolling must be visible in static selectors and covered by Phase-74 smoke.
phase-74-verification-design.gate-3: Protected user frontend, backend, deploy, install, nginx, docker-compose, VPN runtime, and MTProto runtime surfaces are no-diff guards.

V-M-074.phase-74-evidence: Phase-74 smoke passed ResponsiveAdaptation markers ADMIN_SCROLL_NO_OVERLAP and ADMIN_RAIL_VIEWPORT_SAFE, with direct /plans handled as redirect and no protected user/backend/deploy/runtime drift.
V-M-074.phase-75-evidence: Phase-75 smoke passed ResponsiveAdaptation markers UNIFIED_INVENTORY_NO_OVERFLOW and EXPANDED_DEVICES_BOUNDED, with direct /devices handled as redirect and no protected user/backend/deploy/runtime drift.

phase-76-verification-design.scope: Cross-surface responsive regression gate for Phase-72 through Phase-75.
phase-76-verification-design.gate-1: Phone, tablet, desktop, and reduced-motion checks must cover public/auth, user cabinet, and admin cockpit surfaces touched by Phase-72 through Phase-75.
phase-76-verification-design.gate-2: Phase-61 responsive smoke must be run as the shared regression gate after both frontend builds.
phase-76-verification-design.gate-3: Failure conditions include horizontal scroll, content hidden by bottom dock, admin rail/content scroll conflicts, unbounded expanded device lists, or Matrix layer pointer interception.
phase-76-verification-design.commands: node scripts/phase61-responsive-adaptation-smoke.mjs; Phase-72/73 user smokes; Phase-74/75 admin smokes; git diff --check; protected-surface guard.

V-M-074.phase-76-evidence: Consolidated Phase-76 gate passed for responsive adaptation: Phase-61 smoke plus Phase-72/73/74/75 smokes proved phone/tablet/desktop no-overflow, safe bottom dock, admin rail/content scroll safety, and bounded expanded device lists.
